hnmcp - LLM Context Guide

Project purpose
- hnmcp is an AI-native HackerNews MCP server.
- It helps agents retrieve relevant HN stories/comments, evaluate source trust, and return explainable outputs.
- It is designed for agent workflows (query -> tool call -> structured reasoning), not feed browsing.

Architecture: 3 primitives
1) Discover
- Find relevant stories/comments by keyword, score, and time window.
- Uses filtering and ranking signals so results match user intent instead of raw popularity.

2) Trust
- Estimate author credibility from interaction patterns and seed experts.
- Uses EigenTrust-style propagation over reply graphs to surface reliable voices.

3) Understand
- Return explainable quality/trust signals with outputs.
- Emphasizes "why this result" via structured signal breakdowns.

Module responsibilities
- hnmcp/cache.py
  - Async TTL cache with per-key lock deduplication (`get_or_fetch`).
  - Shared caching primitive for network/model-heavy operations.

- hnmcp/profiles.py
  - Loads user profile from JSON or environment fallback.
  - Handles keywords, experts, thresholds, and quality weights.

- hnmcp/quality.py
  - Computes explainable quality signals (practitioner depth, velocity, references, thread depth, expert involvement).
  - Produces weighted quality score from signals.

- hnmcp/trust.py
  - Builds author reply graph from comments.
  - Runs EigenTrust-like iteration and persists trust cache.

- hnmcp/embeddings.py
  - Optional semantic similarity layer via sentence-transformers.
  - Lazy-loads embedding model and caches vectors; no hard dependency unless feature is used.

- hnmcp/server.py
  - FastMCP server entry point (tools/resources wiring).
  - Currently placeholder; implementation should orchestrate Discover/Trust/Understand modules.

Key design decisions
- TDD-first development (tests define behavior before implementation).
- EigenTrust-inspired trust propagation for expert credibility.
- Optional embeddings dependency (install extras only when needed).
- Python 3.9+ compatibility requirement.
- Keep implementation lightweight (no NumPy required in core path).

Quick navigation for AI agents
- Start here for project overview: README.md
- Core package: hnmcp/
  - Cache + concurrency: hnmcp/cache.py
  - User config/profile loading: hnmcp/profiles.py
  - Quality scoring/explainability: hnmcp/quality.py
  - Trust graph + EigenTrust: hnmcp/trust.py
  - Optional embeddings search: hnmcp/embeddings.py
  - MCP tool/resource integration point: hnmcp/server.py
- Behavioral specs/tests: tests/
  - tests/test_cache.py
  - tests/test_profiles.py
  - tests/test_quality.py
  - tests/test_trust.py
  - tests/test_embeddings.py
  - tests/test_server.py

Agent implementation notes
- Prefer async for all external I/O boundaries.
- Preserve explainable outputs (signals + rationale), not opaque scores.
- Reuse cache/trust/quality primitives instead of re-implementing logic in server layer.
